title: Coelorachis clarkei
taxonomy: Coelorachis clarkei is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. The genus Coelorachis is a group of grasses native to tropical and subtropical regions. Coelorachis clarkei is specifically found in Australia, where it grows in open forests and grasslands. This species is also known by its synonyms, including Spinifex clarkei and Spinifex gracilis.

intro: Coelorachis clarkei, commonly known as Clarke's grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelorachis clarkei include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
